Overall:
The following treatment technologies are effective in removing 4-nonylphenol from water: free chlorine (65 to 100 percent), granular activated carbon (up to 90 percent), ozone (37 to 93 percent), ozone combined with peroxide (55 to 90 percent), biological treatment in conjunction with sonochemical treatment (up to 88 percent), and biological treatment (59 to 61 percent). Conventional treatment has shown mixed results. One study reported 10 to 33 percent removal with aluminum sulfate as a coagulant and another study reported no removal when ferric chloride was used. Electrochemical oxidation of 4-nonylphenol has been effective (up to 100 percent) at milligram per liter concentrations.
